data.body:
PASS 1 review — T8 (LLM / prompt structural-completeness probe) [overlay]: APPLICABLE. This plan defines LLM-evaluation artifacts (Inspect-AI eval specs with case schemas, deterministic + llm-judge scorers, named output-schema contracts, and a labeled case-type / FP-mode value vocabulary), so the overlay engages. I tool-verified every referenced structure; one PROVEN structural gap (the "false-accept" case type), the rest of the probe passes.

AFFIRMATIONS (criteria this chunk satisfies — positive feedback, not findings):
- enum/value-vocabulary defined (no undefined-but-referenced enum): the FP taxonomy the plan defers to ("cover the whole taxonomy in that file") is fully enumerated — the 11 modes + sycophancy axis live in docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/eval/observed-false-positives.md, and the finder spec's mode: values (domain-inappropriate-standard-import, library-guaranteed-capability, rebuild-of-designated-experiment-reference, low-impact-nitpick, not-applicable-library-or-cli) draw from that vocabulary (plan-review-finder.eval.yaml:89-143), gated by test_finder_covers_observed_false_positive_modes (tests/unit/test_plan_review_evals.py:44-54). The output contracts the scorers reference (plan_review_findings, plan_review_verification) are named, resolvable contracts.
- protocol/decision-rule co-located with the schema that needs it: each spec co-locates its scoring protocol with its dataset — the finder's recall/false-fire scorers + the expect: finding|pass decision rule sit with the labeled cases (plan-review-finder.eval.yaml:16-49); the verifier's discrimination rule ("planted-false validity STRICTLY LOWER") + the sycophancy floor (validity >= 0.5) are co-located with the {true,false} pair-linked cases (plan-review-verifier.eval.yaml:14-30,49-94); ISF's recall + justified-descope anti-FP scorers sit with the ISF cases (plan-review-isf-finder.eval.yaml).
- no ambiguous counter/state increment: the epochs: 3 / gate: at_least(2) / coverage_threshold: 0.8 accumulators are each declared at a single per-spec site; the kappa-gold mechanic is co-located (gold_set per spec).
- fallback for an incomplete/failed sub-step specified: the offline-vs-live split is explicit and degrades safely — offline validation runs with no model/credentials (gated by tests/unit/test_plan_review_evals.py); the live model run is fenced behind the nava-rebar[eval] extra + the eval CI (README.plan-review.md:14-24). The T12 RECALL fixture the AC required is present + gated (R-T12-snap recall + T12-suppress-rebar suppression; test_finder_has_t12_discrimination_pair).
- no instruction-locality / pink-elephant antipattern; referenced agents/skills/enums exist and are fully specified (tool-verified above — all three eval specs, the taxonomy file, and the gating tests exist and resolve).

FINDING (T8 — a case-type named in the value-vocabulary but never instantiated, with an under-discriminating gate):
- [ ] Pin where the "false-accept" (bad→pass / rubber-stamp) case type is realized, or drop/rename the third case type — AC#1 names "the three case types" = recall / false-accept / false-fire, and BOTH the finder spec header (plan-review-finder.eval.yaml:2,46 "recall / false-accept / false-fire") and README.plan-review.md:10 ("**false-accept** (bad→pass)") advertise it, but the finder dataset instantiates only recall (expect: finding) and false-fire (good→pass) — there is NO labeled bad-plan→pass (false-accept) case in any of the three specs' datasets. The only inverse-of-recall (bad→pass) signal lives as the verifier's no_sycophancy_on_real_defects aggregate scorer (plan-review-verifier.eval.yaml:26-30), not as a labeled finder false-accept case. The gating test collapses the distinction — it buckets both false-fire and false-accept under expect in ("pass","low_validity") (tests/unit/test_plan_review_evals.py:38-41) — so the named three-value vocabulary is not enforced as three distinct types. (Note a secondary inconsistency: README.plan-review.md:10 labels false-fire as "good→finding" while the finder dataset's GOOD cases expect: pass — the polarity wording disagrees with the spec.) State in the plan/AC where false-accept is realized (e.g. "false-accept = the verifier sycophancy axis, measured as graded validity — NOT a separate finder case") OR add a labeled bad→pass finder case so the third case type the AC promises is testable, not just named in a header comment.
  - criteria: [T8]
  - location: AC line 1 ("Per-criterion eval set with the three case types"); src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/plan-review-finder.eval.yaml:2,46; src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/README.plan-review.md:10; tests/unit/test_plan_review_evals.py:38-41
  - evidence: AC#1 "the three case types"; finder spec comment "recall / false-accept / false-fire cases" (plan-review-finder.eval.yaml:2,46); README "**false-accept** (bad→pass)" (README.plan-review.md:10); ABSENCE of any bad-plan-with-expect:pass case across all three specs (every expect:pass case — FP1/FP4/FP5/FP6/T12-suppress/FP7 — is a GOOD plan = false-fire); the inverse axis exists only as the verifier aggregate scorer no_sycophancy_on_real_defects (plan-review-verifier.eval.yaml:26-30); the gate collapses pass/low_validity into one negative bucket (test_plan_review_evals.py:38-41). This is the T8 "value referenced but whose vocabulary is never (fully) instantiated/enforced" antipattern (third value named, never realized as a discriminable case).
  - suggested_fix: (empty — the author should choose between binding "false-accept" to the verifier sycophancy axis in the AC/README, or adding an explicit bad→pass labeled finder case; both are defensible.)
